what_it_does: A standalone HTML preview card specimen for the monospace typeface (JetBrains Mono): shows a URL specimen with coloured path segments, a dark code-block sample with a CLI command, and a metadata strip of font/weights/usage.
observations: Carries a @dsCard annotation: group='Brand', name='Mono — JetBrains Mono', subtitle='URLs, IDs, code blocks', viewport='700x220'.
URL specimen colour-codes path segments: gold accent for 'acme-arch', muted for 'tower-east'.
Code block on --bg-dark with inverse text shows a CLI command: 'conceptv publish --hub tower-east --stage entry' with a gold $ prompt.
standouts: Sample content uses real ConceptV constructs: conceptv.io panotours URL and a 'conceptv publish' CLI command with hub/stage flags.
Uses a distinct --accent-gold-dashed token for the code-block prompt key, separate from --accent-gold.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard gallery convention and var()-token discipline, including a radius token (--r-md) and dark-surface tokens.
Specimen font sizes hardcoded as illustrative values rather than via scale tokens.
notes: JetBrains Mono specimen card; a colour-segmented URL and a dark CLI code block, declaring mono's role for URLs/IDs/code.
questions: What distinguishes --accent-gold from --accent-gold-dashed in the token registry?
Is 'conceptv publish --hub … --stage …' a real CLI command in the app or illustrative?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard names it the JetBrains Mono specimen and that is what renders.
intention_signals: Intended as a recognisable mono specimen showing how URLs, IDs, and code are styled, including path-segment colour coding and code-block treatment.
novelty: The path-segment colour coding (gold/muted) of a URL and the CLI-command code block as a styling demonstration.
contribution: Contributes the canonical monospace specimen (JetBrains Mono) plus the URL-segment colouring pattern and dark code-block treatment with its dedicated tokens.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML @dsCard preview specimen for JetBrains Mono as the design system's monospace typeface, showing a colour-segmented URL, a dark CLI code block, and weight/usage metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To give an eye-recognisable reference for the monospace typeface and how URLs, IDs, and code blocks are styled.
